meaning: adv.疯子似地,摇摇晃晃地,发狂地
examples: The sail of the little boat swung crazily from one side to the other.
Beams from the flashlights bounced crazily among the trees, and the search party crashed through the undergrowth, stumbling over stumps and fallen logs.
The ball bounced crazily over his shoulder into the net.
Crazily, that the potential revenue stream is unknown actually makes Project Glass more appealing to Google Ventures.
But crazily enough, Page and Brin elected to do a very maverick thing when it came to financing Google.
